selected_important_turn.text: I was thinking S’mores!  I will start off....2 firewood, 2 water, 1 food
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete allocation from you and it anchors the negotiation toward a competitive, zero-sum frame by claiming the majority of the scarce top-priority item (firewood). It substantially shaped subsequent offers and the bargaining space.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The move is overly claim-oriented and rigid: it asserts an allocation favorable to you without offering tradeable concessions or explaining priorities. That escalates conflict over firewood and misses an early chance to open integrative trade options (e.g., linking firewood for food or water).
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the anchor and create a conditional, tradeable offer or disclose priorities: signal willingness to trade portions of firewood for more of an item the other values, or present the proposal as a starting point and invite alternatives. Add rationale for the requested split to make it easier to trade.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase (if you open conditional trades instead of rigid claims)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (more transparent trade options make outcomes feel less like grabs)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (reduces immediate defensiveness and opens concessions)

value2reason.Low: I am fluffy and I like food
value2reason.High: I would like a campfire to make the perfect evening

value2reason.Low: Perhaps the place I am camping is in a place with running water (streams, springs, pumps). Also, I am sure I packed beer and bottled water in my camping supplies to drink and wash dishes with already.
value2reason.Medium: Other people in the camping trip (or myself) may get really hungry certain nights. Having more food is therefore necessary for me to have an enjoyable camping experience!
value2reason.High: It may get cold and firewood can be hard to come by at certain campsites. Plus, firewood is needed for cooking and making plenty of Smores! The extra firewood can be consumed the last night with a big fire so I do not have to take extra home with me.

dialogue.text: Hi there! Hope your day is going well 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Pretty good, Monday's are always bit of a struggle though!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Tell me about it!  With this pandemic it seems like all days are Mondays 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Too true! Anyways, what is the item that you prefer the most for this camping adventure? 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Oh, firewood as well! It's not a camping trip if you run out of firewood in just a few nights! I would hate to be cold
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I was thinking S’mores!  I will start off....2 firewood, 2 water, 1 food
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’d like to propose a split: 2 Firewood, 2 Water, and 1 Food for me, and the remaining 1 Firewood, 1 Water, and 2 Food for you. Firewood is my top priority, but I’m open to trading some for more water or food if it makes sense for you.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I value firewood highly. How about 2 Firewood, 1 Food, and 0 Water for me, and 1 Firewood, 2 Food, and 3 Water for you? It aligns better with my priorities while ensuring fair distribution.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your preference for firewood. Since it's my top priority, I’m willing to adjust. How about 1 Firewood, 3 Water, and 1 Food for me, and 2 Firewood, 0 Water, and 2 Food for you? It balances our priorities and opens room for trade.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm happy with the split. Let's confirm: 2 Firewood, 1 Food, and 0 Water for me, and 1 Firewood, 2 Food, and 3 Water for you. It matches my priorities and ensures a fair deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
